tool_args.label: ADA at 149th Street - Grand Concourse and Tremont Avenue
tool_args.raw_text: The project will provide ADA accessibility to three underground subway stations 149 St-Grand Concourse complex (2, 4, 5) and Tremont Av (B, D). The project will also accommodate free ADA transfers between subway lines at 149 St-Grand Concourse Station.

payload.project_name: ADA at 149th Street - Grand Concourse and Tremont Avenue
payload.description: Design-build contract for installation of six new elevators, stairs, elevator machine rooms, and ADA boarding areas at 149 St-Grand Concourse Station (2, 4, 5) and Tremont Av Station (B, D). Includes restoration and re-opening of the historic headhouse at 149 St station. Contract numbers A37131, A37134. Budget $163M. Substantial completion forecast July 2023.

tool_args.label: ADA Package 2 - Accessibility Upgrade at Eight Stations
tool_args.raw_text: This bundled project is a design-build contract that will install new ADA compliant elevators at six (6) stations, replacement of elevators at two (2) stations and state of good repair (SoGR) work at two (2) of the new ADA stations.

payload.project_name: ADA Package 2 - Accessibility Upgrade at Eight Stations
payload.description: Design-build contract providing 16 elevators (11 new, 5 replacement) at eight stations: 8th Avenue (N), Westchester Square (6), 181st Street (A), Woodhaven Boulevard (J,Z), Court Square (G), Queensboro Plaza (N,W,7), Marcy Avenue (J,M,Z), Flushing Avenue (J,M,Z). Budget $471M. Substantial completion September 2024.

tool_args.label: ADA at 68th Street/Hunter College
tool_args.raw_text: This project is a design-build contract to provide ADA accessibility, including elevators and other improvements, at 68th Street/Hunter College Station on the Lexington Avenue Line (6) in the Borough of Manhattan.

payload.description: Design-build contract providing three new elevators, two new street-to-platform entrances, track and platform edge rehabilitation, and SoGR upgrades at 68th Street/Hunter College Station (Lexington Avenue Line 6). Budget $177M. Substantial completion December 2024.

tool_args.label: Flushing Line Improvements Design-Build
tool_args.raw_text: Design-Build Services for Improvements on the Flushing Line - Contract A46030 with Skanska Railroad Construction Joint Venture, $199,955,000

payload.description: State of good repair improvements at 61st St-Woodside Station, replacement of existing escalators at 61st St-Woodside and 74th Street-Broadway Stations, and painting and repair of structural defects between 48th Street and 72nd Street along the Flushing Line. Contract A46030, $199,955,000, 980 calendar days.

warnings: project.lead_agency is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Lead agency is relation context. Suggested relation: implemented_by.

tool_args.label: Escalator Replacement at 6 Stations Systemwide
tool_args.raw_text: Design-build services to replace seventeen (17) escalators at six (6) subway stations systemwide - Contract E30641 with Skanska USA Civil Northeast, Inc., $114,970,000

payload.description: Replacement of 17 escalators at 6 subway stations in the Bronx, Brooklyn, and Manhattan. Includes installation of new escalator equipment within existing machine rooms and replacement of associated electrical, mechanical, and plumbing equipment. Contract E30641, $114,970,000, 1,447 days.

warnings: project.lead_agency is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Lead agency is relation context. Suggested relation: implemented_by.

tool_args.label: QMT Refueling Station and QSB Switchgear Relocation
tool_args.raw_text: Design-build services for relocation of Queens Midtown Tunnel refueling station and Queens Service Building switchgear - Contract QM-36 B/C with E-J Electric Installation Company, $25,450,000

payload.project_name: QMT Refueling Station and QSB Switchgear Relocation
payload.description: Removal of existing refueling station from QSB garage on 51st Avenue in Long Island City, installation of new outdoor vehicle refueling station and EV charging stations at Borden Avenue lot. Construction of new electrical distribution room in QSB mezzanine. Contract QM-36B/C, $25,450,000, 2 years 2 months.

warnings: project.lead_agency is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Lead agency is relation context. Suggested relation: implemented_by.

tool_args.label: R211 Subway Car Program
tool_args.raw_text: The R211 program supports these goals by replacing the 40+ year-old R46s with CBTC-equipped cars to enable CBTC expansion on the B-Division, including the 8th Avenue A, C, and E Lines. The R211s will also replace the Staten Island Railway R44 cars.

payload.description: R211 contract awarded to Kawasaki in February 2018 for up to 1,612 cars for NYCT and Staten Island Railway. Base order: 535 cars, budgeted at $1.75B, comprising 440 closed-end cars, 75 SIR cars, and 20 open-gangway test cars. Replacing R46 (40+ years old) and SIR R44 (close to 50 years old) cars. Forecast delivery of all vehicles moved from July 2023 to January 2025 (18-month delay).

warnings: project.agency is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Agency names should link to entity records when supported. Suggested relation: implemented_by.

tool_args.label: LIRR M-9 Railcar Project
tool_args.raw_text: The M-9 cars will provide passenger service to Grand Central Madison, allowing retirement of M-3 cars. M-9 contract awarded to Kawasaki Railcar in Yonkers, NY in September 2013, initial base order of 92 cars with an option exercised for additional 110 cars in July 2017 at combined budget of approximately $734M.

payload.description: M-9 railcar procurement from Kawasaki Railcar. Base order of 92 cars plus option for 110 additional cars (exercised July 2017). Combined budget approximately $734M. Cars will serve Grand Central Madison, allowing retirement of M-3 cars. Forecast conditional acceptance by September 2023, total delay of 33 months. Production averaging 4 cars/month, needs to increase to 5 cars/month for final 72 vehicles.

warnings: project.agency is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Agency names should link to entity records when supported. Suggested relation: implemented_by.
project.operator is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Operator is an entity relation, not a project enum. Suggested relation: operated_by.

tool_args.label: Metro-North SC42 Locomotive Contract
tool_args.raw_text: $378M contract (including base and options), awarded to Siemens in March 2021, is for 27 locomotives to replace the aged P-32s.

payload.description: $378M contract (including base and options) awarded to Siemens March 2021 for 27 locomotives to replace P-32s. On schedule with first locomotive delivery in mid-2025, last locomotive two years later. EPA Tier IV compliant.

warnings: project.agency is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Agency names should link to entity records when supported. Suggested relation: implemented_by.

tool_args.label: ADA Package 1 - Accessibility Upgrades at Eight Stations
tool_args.raw_text: The ADA Stations - Package 1 Design-Build contract will provide accessibility at 8 subway stations located across the five boroughs.

payload.project_name: ADA Package 1 - Accessibility Upgrades at Eight Stations
payload.description: Design-build contract awarded to Judlau Contracting Inc. (JCI) and STV in December 2020. Provides ADA accessibility at New Dorp (SIR), Grand St. (L), Beach 67 St (A), 7 Av (F,G), Dyckman St northbound only (1), East 149 St (6), Lorimer St (L), and Metropolitan Av (G) stations. 18 new elevators (12 machine-room-less, 6 hydraulic). Budget $324M. Substantial completion July 2023.

tool_args.raw_text: June 27, 2022 meeting of the MTA Capital Program Committee, New York, New York, 12:15 P.M.

payload.description: MTA Capital Program Committee meeting held on June 27, 2022

tool_args.label: MTA New Bus Program
tool_args.raw_text: Mr. Cardoza provided an overview of the current bus fleet, which consists of approximately 5,800 buses -- with an average fleet age of 7.89 years -- at 28 bus depots city-wide. All contracts for the 1,776 buses funded in the 2015-2019 Capital Program have been awarded. 7 contracts awarded for 706 buses for the 2020-2024 Capital Program. Plans to solicit 470 Battery-Electric Buses by end of this year.

payload.description: Bus fleet procurement program. Current fleet of approximately 5,800 buses at 28 depots city-wide. 1,776 buses from 2015-2019 Capital Program awarded (89% accepted). 706 buses from 2020-2024 Capital Program under contract. Plans to solicit 470 Battery-Electric Buses (including 90 articulated) by end of 2022. All contracts on budget.

warnings: project.agency is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Agency names should link to entity records when supported. Suggested relation: implemented_by.

tool_args.label: Rutgers Tube Rehabilitation - 53rd Street Tube Modifications
tool_args.raw_text: Contract P36444 is for the Rutgers Tube rehabilitation. Modifications No. 41, 42, and 43 to address deterioration and corrosion of electrical systems and equipment for the 53rd Street Tube.

payload.description: Modifications to Contract P36444 (Rutgers Tube rehabilitation) to address deterioration and corrosion of electrical systems in the 53rd Street Tube between Lexington Avenue/53rd Street Station (Manhattan) and Court Square-23rd Street Station (Queens). Mod. 41 ($1,275,000) replaces electrical system at Nott Avenue. Mod. 42 ($2,948,820) installs 480-volt cables and pumps at Roosevelt Island. Mod. 43 ($1,155,000) replaces switchgear at Sutton Place. Total $5,378,820.

tool_args.label: 41st Street Substation Restoration
tool_args.raw_text: Contract P-36700 Modification No. 11 for replacement of existing transformer and rectifier equipment at the 41st Street Substation in Queens. $3,775,000

payload.description: Restoration of service at 41st Street Substation in Astoria, Queens, providing DC power to the Queens Boulevard Line (QBL). Removal and replacement of transformer and rectifier equipment. Modification No. 11 to Contract P-36700 for $3,775,000. Revised substantial completion date of February 29, 2024. Needed to equalize power loads and restore redundancy for CBTC system power requirements.

tool_args.label: Six New Elevators at 149th Street/Grand Concourse and Tremont Ave
tool_args.raw_text: 6 new elevators and a free accessible station transfer will provide access between the White Plains line and Jerome line at 149th Street.

payload.description: Six new elevators at 149th Street/Grand Concourse and Tremont Avenue stations for ADA accessibility. Elevators 509, 510, 511 at Tremont Ave; Elevators 101, 102 at 149th St-Grand Concourse; plus headhouse elevator.

tool_args.label: 18 New Elevators at 8 Stations (ADA Package 1)
tool_args.raw_text: Installation of a total of 18 new elevators throughout all 8 stations, of which 12 new elevators will be machine room-less.

payload.description: 18 new elevators (12 machine-room-less, 6 hydraulic) across 8 stations in all five boroughs. Modern Elevator is manufacturer with 15-year maintenance contract.

tool_args.label: Three New Elevators at 68th Street/Hunter College
tool_args.raw_text: The project provides three (3) new hydraulic elevators, two (2) new stairs (street to platform), two (2) reconfigured stairs (street to mezzanine) as well as track, platform edge rehabilitation and other State of Good Repair (SoGR) upgrades.

payload.description: Three new hydraulic elevators: Elevator 286 (street to mezzanine), Elevator 284 (to southbound platform), Elevator 285 (to northbound platform). Plus two new street-to-platform entrances.

tool_args.label: R211 Program to Use Lessons Learned from LIRR M-9
tool_args.raw_text: Acknowledging the LIRR Project Team's close supervision of Kawasaki to bring the quality of the M-9's to an acceptable level, the R211 Program is expected to benefit from this experience and will utilize lessons learned to avoid quality issues.

payload.claim_text: R211 Program expected to benefit from LIRR M-9 experience and utilize lessons learned to avoid quality issues.
